Date: Thu, 23 Apr 2026 01:36:01 +0800
Subject: Critical UX Issue: Incorrect Initial Page Render and Transition Jitter
============ Forwarded message ============

Hi Team,

I have identified a significant regression in the site’s navigation logic across the Wellness, Bath, and Hardware categories. The current user journey is being disrupted by a "ghosting" effect and inconsistent loading states.

Identified Issues

  • Incorrect Initial Render (Page Overlapping): Upon selecting a category, the browser initially renders an unrelated product listing page (refer to the attached screenshot) before eventually redirecting to the correct content. This suggests a potential issue with the client-side routing or an improperly cached state that persists before the new view is fully mounted.

  • Navigation Jitter: The transition between pages is lacking smoothness. We are seeing high Layout Shift (CLS) and stuttering during the fetch/render cycle, which degrades the perceived performance of the site.

  • Latency in Final Payload: There is a noticeable delay between the initial (incorrect) paint and the final (correct) page load, suggesting that the logic for resolving the destination URL may be bogged down by redundant requests or heavy script execution.

Impact

This creates a confusing user experience that feels unpolished and may lead to high bounce rates, as users are initially presented with irrelevant data.

Supporting Documentation

  • Screenshot: Attached (showing the incorrect initial state).

  • Screen Recording: Attached (demonstrating the jitter and the "flash" of the overlapping page).


--
Thank you,

name: AI Search questions
description:
Hi team,

Please see this query from the client:

I've been using Claude to build out new page verbiage and SEO-driven image alt descriptions, and while I had it in there, I asked it to review the site for any disconnects I might have left behind. It came back with a list tied to how search is changing now that Google leans on AI to answer questions and cite sources directly.

All of this is over my head, so I'm just forwarding it to you for review. Here's what it flagged:

Rendering. The raw HTML of our pages shows a fully populated head, but the body is essentially empty until the JavaScript renders. It said this is normal for our framework and that real browsers handle it fine, but raised questions about whether AI crawlers that don't render JavaScript are seeing our content, and whether SSR or prerendering makes sense for the category and blog pages.

Blog content. It noted that if these are rendered client-side only, they may not reach the engines that now summarize and cite, and suggested confirming what a non-rendering crawler actually sees there.

Images. It flagged alt text, filenames, image sitemaps, and image structured data as the levers that make images discoverable.

Metadata. It found a few category pages falling back to just "Studio Belmont" instead of page-specific titles and descriptions, and guessed it's likely a per-page CMS field or template thing.

Structured data. It looked at how we're set up for JSON-LD (Organization, LocalBusiness, Product, Article, Breadcrumb) and noted that facts like founded 1938, six showrooms, family-owned are exactly what the AI answers pull from.

No idea what's already handled, what any of it means, or what actually matters here, which is why I'm passing it to the experts.

Do these need to be corrected? In the images section, is this something I am not filling out appropriately?

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you!

name: #20429 Your Buffalo Lawyer
description:
Hey team,

We have a critical SEO issue on yourbuffalolawyer.com that needs to be fixed this week. Every page on the site has its canonical tag pointing to the homepage URL instead of the actual page URL. This is causing Google to treat 145+ pages as duplicates of the homepage and drop them from the index entirely.

The bug:

If you view source on any inner page (for example /vehicle-accidents/car-accident/), you'll see:

<link rel="canonical" href="https://yourbuffalolawyer.com/" />

It should be:

<link rel="canonical" href="https://yourbuffalolawyer.com/vehicle-accidents/car-accident/" />

This is happening on every page across the site. The og:url and twitter:url meta tags have the same problem.

The fix:

In the Next.js head/SEO component, the canonical URL needs to be built dynamically from the current page slug instead of defaulting to the homepage. Search the codebase for rel="canonical" to find where it's being set. The slug is already available from Sanity, it just needs to be passed through. Pattern should be:

const canonicalUrl = \https://yourbuffalolawyer.com/${slug}/\`;`

Then use that value for the canonical tag, og:url, and twitter:url.

Rules to follow:

  • Always use the full absolute URL with trailing slash
  • Never fall back to the homepage URL if the slug is empty (that's a bug, not a fallback)
  • Homepage should keep its own canonical as https://yourbuffalolawyer.com/
  • Make sure there's only one canonical tag per page (no duplicates from layout + page)

How to verify:

View source on a few inner pages after deploying and confirm the canonical href matches the actual page URL. Check a practice area page, an attorney page, and a blog post.

Once this is live, let me know and we'll re-submit the key pages for indexing in Google Search Console.


Bob Flavin
Executive Vice President

Direct Line: 616-886-5615

name: Christmas Menu Page
description:
Please create a new page with a simple form with the following details:
​
English Content:
Christmas Menu – by reservation (16, 17 & 18 December)
In the week leading up to Christmas, we will be serving a special 3-course Christmas lunch menu alongside our regular selection.

Lunch menu €46.50 p.p. (3 courses)
Smoked salmon tartare, apple salad, beetroot, and horseradish cream
Thinly sliced veal sirloin with Puy lentils, winter vegetables, and truffle sauce
Tiramisu

It is possible to add a soup course. In that case, the menu price is €55.

A dinner in the Red Room, Green Room, or the Vault is also possible. These events can be arranged and reserved on request, due to the required customization.

To ensure proper preparation and availability, we kindly ask you to reserve the Christmas lunch in the Brasserie in advance.

Please send us your reservations no later than Friday 12 December at 17:00.
For evening events or special occasions, we kindly ask you to notify us at least 5 days in advance.

Dutch Content:
Kerstmenu – op reservering (16, 17 & 18 december) In de week voorafgaand aan kerst serveren wij, naast onze reguliere assortiment, een speciaal 3-gangen kerstmenu tijdens de lunch.
Lunchmenu € 46,50 p.p. (3 gangen)
Tartaar van gerookte zalm, salade van appel, bietjes en mierikswortel crème Dungesneden kalfslende met pui linzen, wintergroente en truffelsaus Tiramisu

Mogelijk uit te breiden met soep als tussengerecht. In dat geval bedraagt de prijs van het menu € 55,- Een diner in de Rode- of Groene Kamer of in de Kluis behoort zeker tot de mogelijkheden.
Dergelijke evenementen kunnen op aanvraag worden gereserveerd en georganiseerd, vanwege het noodzakelijk maatwerk. Het heeft de voorkeur in verband met voorbereidingen en beschikbaarheid om de kerstlunch in de Brasserie vooraf te reserveren
Wij verzoeken u vriendelijk om uiterlijk vrijdag 12 december vóór 17:00 uur uw reserveringen door te geven. Voor het reserveren van een avond evenement of een speciale gelegenheid vragen wij om 5 dagen voorafgaand aan het evenement in acht te nemen.
​

Let me know if there are any questions. Thank you!

name: #19927 Promotion Pricing Issue: CTG
description:
Hello Webriq Team,

 

URGENT: We have hit a snag with pricing on the portal for Cabinets to Go (CTG).

 

The CTG discount structure is unique to each brand that they access.

                BK = 50-10 (multiplier .45)

                HH = 50-10-10-5 (multiplier .38475)

 

 

Example Part P3012-SN

MSRP

CTG Rate

CTG Cost 

Promo Discount

Final CTG Cost

$26.99

.385

$10.39

20%

$8.32

 

However, on the portal their final unit cost is $9.72.

 

It looks like the BK discount structure is applied to this HH part.

$26.99 x .45 = $12.15  |  $12.15 – 20% = $9.72

 

Please correct the pricing for CTG and make sure the correct discount structure is applied for each brand.

 

Let me know if you have any questions.

Thanks.

Improving Mobile Form Responsiveness 📱 - Watch Video

In this video, I discuss the issues with form submissions on mobile devices due to improper responsiveness settings in your website builder. Hello everyone! My name is Dr. Ben Farca. I was born and raised in Queens NY and moved down to Marietta GA to attend Life University where I graduated with my doctor of chiropractic degree. During my time at school I specialized in the Blair Upper Cervical technique and have continued to utilize this method in treating patients. The reason I became an upper cervical chiropractor is because upper cervical chiropractic saved my life. After experiencing a few concussion when I was younger playing sports as well as another more severe one in college I was left with extreme brain fog, migraines, neck pain, low back pain, and was getting cold symptoms 1-2 times a month, as well as many other neurological issues. The doctors that I spoke with told me there were no solutions to post concussion syndrome and my scans that I have received all came back clear. I was told to take medications for my migraines and cold symptoms and throughout this time I began to lose all motivation to be in school and struggled with terrible memory issues and low energy levels. 


After beginning my chiropractic journey I started to receive upper cervical chiropractic care and after my first adjustment I slept around 14 hours that night and woke up the following morning experiencing no symptoms at all. I finally felt clear! This was an eye opening experience for me and I felt determined to become an upper cervical chiropractor myself and change the lives of many just like mine was. Looking forward to serving you in St. Augustine FL!

Hi Danessa,

Please add Dr. Nicole King to spineoflifechiro.com website. Bio and picture below.


Dr. Nicole King, your upper neck specialist here in Saint Augustine Florida, has always been active. She spent part of her childhood on her dad’s farm, running barefoot through pastures, playing with cows, and learning the value of movement and hard work early on. Later, she moved to Virginia Beach and fully embraced the beach lifestyle.

Her love for health and performance led her to Virginia Tech, where she earned her Bachelor's degree in Human Nutrition, Foods, and Exercise. It opened her eyes to how powerfully lifestyle choices shape our long term wellbeing, and kickstarted her passion for preventative care.

Dr. Nicole experienced an injury from cross country running that led her to pursue chiropractic, but severe brain fog and strange health symptoms during chiropractic school lead her to seek out the Blair upper cervical technique. Symptoms included talking to friends she knew for years and not remembering their names, walking but not realizing where she was walking to, and visualizing words she wanted to write but would be unable to get them on paper. After receiving Blair upper cervical chiropractic adjustments she no longer experiences any of these brain fog symptoms. She decided to dedicate her life to the precise, gentle, and specific technique that changed her own. 

Outside the office, you’ll find Dr. Nicole in the gym training for physique competitions, meal prepping, or out in nature. She lives and breathes the holistic and healthy lifestyle she helps her patients create.

Now, Dr. Nicole has the absolute honor of serving the Saint Augustine community, and her loved ones, with the same care that gave her life back. She believes in getting better, and then getting even better, and that’s the energy she brings to every patient she meets. Let’s get you back to your 100%.

description:
Hi Team,

We have a mutual client, Dr. Pecca from Spine of Life Upper Cervical Chiropractic, who will be using our web tools and needs them added to their website.

Their Launch Call is scheduled for 4/23/2026.

Please do not add the 24/7 Online Scheduler or the "Schedule Appointment" button yet.

  • Why? Publishing the scheduler button before the setup is finalized creates a poor patient experience. Patients will see the option to schedule, but because the tool isn't ready, they will hit a dead end. We need to avoid this confusion.

Action Item for AFTER the Launch Call: Once the launch call is complete, please finalize the setup by adding the 24/7 Online Scheduler code below and placing a "Schedule Appointment" button at the top of the navigation menu.

Website: www.spineoflifechiro.com

Here are a few examples for reference:

  • Example 1
  • Example 2 
  • Example 3

TEXTING COMPLIANCE EDITS 🛡️

The next critical step for the client is to update their website to meet texting compliance standards. Without these modifications, their texting registration will not be approved, and they will be unable to utilize SMS communication. Please begin working on these website updates as soon as possible to ensure timely approval.

  • Privacy Policy Page: This page must also be readily accessible on the website (typically in the footer also) with information on how a person's information will be used, along with a statement stating that Mobile Opt In data will not be shared, sold, or rented. Please CLICK HERE for further details and a compliant example you are welcome to use.

name: #20673 JVCP website change
description:
Hi April! 

Here I am again. Could you please change something on the website again? I have listed the items below, let me know if anything is not clear. 

On the page "Vereniging" there need to be some changes of people. Some left, some are new. 

The person's in the list that can be removed (photo + text) are: Kaj Brouwers, Nadine Hillberg. 

And 3 new members can be uploaded with photo and text: 

  1. Inge Veldhuizen. 
She has no photo, so just a picture like Tijn has (grey). 
Text: Na het behalen van mijn bull aan het Maastricht UMC+ in 2019 ben ik vertrokken naar het Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center in New York, waar ik mijn promotieonderzoek heb verricht. Ik heb mij  gericht op gelaatsreconstructies na huidkanker, een onderwerp dat de basis heeft gevormd voor mijn verdere interesse binnen de plastische chirurgie. Na een jaar in New York ben ik gaan werken als ANIOS Plastische Chirurgie in het Slingeland Ziekenhuis in Doetinchem. In deze periode heb ik mijn promotietraject afgerond, wat resulteerde in de succesvolle verdediging van mijn proefschrift “Facial Scars: Changing Quality of Life” op 10 december 2021. Vanaf maart 2022 ben ik werkzaam geweest als ANIOS Plastische Chirurgie in het Catharina Ziekenhuis in Eindhoven. In maart 2023 kreeg ik het mooie nieuws dat ik was aangenomen voor de opleiding tot plastisch chirurg. Voorafgaand aan de start van mijn opleiding ben ik nog voor zes maanden teruggekeerd naar het Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center voor een post-doc, waarna ik in September 2023 ben gestart met de opleiding. Ik vind het nog altijd leuk en uitdagend om klinisch werk en onderzoek te combineren. Zo ben ik copromotor van meerdere promovendi en begeleid ik geneeskundestudenten bij hun onderzoeksprojecten. Sinds september 2025 ben ik daarnaast penningmeester binnen het bestuur van de JVPC.

  1. Jamilla Wedefoort 
Photo: attached as Jamilla Wedefoort

Text: In 2011 verhuisde ik van Curaçao naar België om tot 2018 geneeskunde te studeren aan de KU Leuven, waar ik voor het eerst in aanraking kwam met reconstructieve chirurgie. Na mijn studies werkte ik tijdelijk als ANIOS bij CTC in Nieuwegein, waarna ik besloot naar het zuiden te verhuizen om daar mijn droom te volgen en als ANIOS Plastische Chirurgie te werken. In de jaren die volgden werkte ik als arts-onderzoeker en promoveerde ik in 2023 op autologe borstreconstructie middels AFT. Gedurende deze tijd had ik ook het geluk om enkele maanden stage te lopen in Miami (Miami Breast Center). Sinds 2024 ben ik in opleiding tot plastisch chirurg in het Maastricht UMC+.

  1. Eva Meier, 
Photo: attached as EvaMeier 
Text: In 2012 ben ik begonnen aan de studie Geneeskunde in Nijmegen, waar al vroeg in de studie mijn interesse voor de Plastische Chirurgie ontstond. Tijdens mijn master ben ik begonnen met wetenschappelijk onderzoek, waarbij ik mij richtte op Hyperbare Zuurstof Therapie (HBOT) bij late radiatieschade van de borst en borstreconstructies. Na het behalen van mijn artsenbul ben ik werkzaam geweest als ANIOS Chirurgie in het Gelre Ziekenhuis in Apeldoorn, en later als ANIOS Plastische Chirurgie in het CWZ in Nijmegen. In 2022 ben ik gestart in het Radboudumc als ANIOS en promovendus. Mijn promotieonderzoek richt zich op het preoperatief opsporen van perforatoren van vrije lappen (oa DIEP, ALT. PAP) met behulp van Projected Augmented Reality. In 2024 ben ik met veel enthousiasme aangenomen voor de opleiding tot Plastisch Chirurg. Tot op heden combineer ik mijn klinische werkzaamheden met mijn promotietraject.

And on the main page: 
Could you please remove the logo of Aleamed, The perfect surgical match? (purple)
We will provide another logo in return but I have to wait until they send me their logo. But Aleamed can be removed already. 

Thank you April and let me know if anything is unclear! 

Met vriendelijke groet,

 

Drs. Eva Meier
AIOS Plastische Chirurgie
